excerpt: The Federal Funds Rate was near zero in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, then gradually increased to around 0.25% in 2026. The Federal Reserve has been adjusting rates to manage economic conditions since 1990.

content.introduction: The Federal Funds Rate was near zero in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic, then gradually increased to around 0.25% in 2026. The Federal Reserve has been adjusting rates to manage economic conditions since 1990.
body: The Federal Reserve will meet on Wednesday and set interest rates. The federal funds rate is the main lever the Federal Reserve uses to

Interest rates are the Federal Reserve’s most powerful lever for steering the U.S. economy — and after years of keeping borrowing costs at their highest level in decades, the Fed has finally been reversing course. Like a chain of dominoes falling one by one, a rate cut (or hike) from the Fed translates to cheaper (or more expensive) borrowing costs on almost every purchase a consumer finances: from credit card spending and auto loans to mortgages and home equity lines of credit. Besides during the early 1990s, the Fed mainly adjusted rates at Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings, a practice that is in rhythm with today’s Fed. Officials did hike rates on April 19, 1994, at an emergency meeting due to inflation worries, and they cut borrowing costs at an unscheduled Oct. 15, 1998, gathering. The Fed then did the unthinkable: It slashed interest rates by 100 basis points to near-zero.

## Fed Funds Interest Rate (1954-2026). Markets Metals Energy Commodities Interest Rates Exchange Rates Economy. 10Y Treasury Fed Funds Rate 1Y Treasury 5Y Treasury 30Y Treasury 30Y Mortgage 2Y Yield TED Spread. Interactive chart showing the current and historical daily level of the U.S. federal funds interest rate back to 1954. The fed funds rate is the interest rate at which depository institutions (banks and credit unions) lend reserve balances to other depository institutions overnight, on an uncollateralized basis. The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meets eight times a year to determine the federal funds target rate. The full historical dataset is available for download here: Fed Funds Interest Rate | Data | 1954-2026. ## Historical Chart.
